title: HASC NDAA markup challenges Space Force on satellite programs
summary: The House Armed Services Committee (HASC) is challenging the U.S. Space Force by preserving a missile-warning satellite program that the Pentagon had planned to cancel. It also criticizes recent tactical communications satellite procurement and GPS oversight.

title: AstroForge completes DeepSpace-2 spacecraft
summary: Asteroid mining startup AstroForge has completed assembly of its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft for a planned launch later this year. The company incorporated lessons from a failed mission in 2023 to improve the new spacecraft.

title: Delian Asparouhov and Philip Johnston on making the case for orbital data centers
summary: The article discusses a SpaceNews event where Jeff Foust interviews Delian Asparouhov of Founders Fund about the potential of orbital data centers. The conversation highlights reasons why Asparouhov is increasingly supportive of this concept.

title: NASA working to streamline development of nuclear electric propulsion demo mission
summary: NASA is implementing a streamlined management approach to accelerate the development of a nuclear electric propulsion demonstration mission, aiming for a launch within two and a half years.

title: China builds institutional framework for space computing push
summary: China is creating an industrial policy framework to support the development of space-based computing infrastructure, with new coordinating bodies emerging to drive this initiative.

title: SpaceX to raise at least $75 billion in IPO
summary: SpaceX plans to raise at least $75 billion through an IPO, valuing the company at over $1.75 trillion. This marks one of the largest public offerings in corporate history.

title: NASA declares end to MAVEN Mars mission
summary: NASA has officially ended the MAVEN Mars mission after losing contact with the spacecraft for six months. The investigation into the spacecraft's demise is still ongoing.

title: AST SpaceMobile sees New Glenn setback delaying initial commercial service into 2027
summary: AST SpaceMobile's initial commercial service for its direct-to-smartphone constellation is delayed into the first half of 2027 due to Blue Origin's New Glenn launchpad explosion. The setback is expected to push the timeline by three to six months.

title: After cooperation on SMILE mission, ESA and China chart parallel but separate paths
summary: ESA and China successfully launched the joint SMILE magnetosphere mission after a decade of cooperation, but future collaborations remain unlikely despite shared goals.

title: Electronics manufacturer Murata to explore Xona satellite timing service for telecom, data centers
summary: Murata is evaluating Xona's satellite timing service to enhance reliability for telecom and data center operations. Xona is building a low Earth orbit constellation to provide GPS backup solutions.

title: Glittering star cluster image reveals missing patch of stars: 'We were not looking for the gap, but we found it'
summary: The Euclid space telescope discovered an unexpected gap in the brightness distribution of red dwarf stars within the ancient globular cluster NGC 6397. Researchers noted they were not specifically searching for this gap but stumbled upon it during observations.

title: August 2026 lunar eclipse: Everything you need to know about the 96% 'blood moon'
summary: A deep partial lunar eclipse, known as the 'blood moon,' will occur on August 27-28, 2026, and will be visible in North America. It will be the best lunar eclipse visible on Earth until New Year's Eve 2028.

title: SpaceX launches back-to-back Starlink missions from both coasts 19 hours apart (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched two Starlink missions from California and Florida within 19 hours, deploying over 50 new satellites into orbit.

title: 12 of the weirdest 'Masters of the Universe' characters
summary: The article humorously lists 12 unusual characters from the 'Masters of the Universe' franchise, highlighting their bizarre traits and origins. It contrasts them with the oddities of the Mos Eisley Cantina from Star Wars.

title: Most exoplanets might be 'soot factories,' scientists say: 'Like you have a natural diesel engine'
summary: Scientists suggest that most exoplanets might function as 'soot factories,' with atmospheres resembling combustion engine emissions. The discovery was inspired by a chemical engineer's observation of spectral similarities between mini-Neptune planets and soot from engines.

title: NASA's dead Mars orbiter MAVEN will crash into the Red Planet in the next 100 years. It's not the only probe in the Mars morgue
summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars orbiter has been declared dead after 12 years of service and is expected to crash into Mars within the next 100 years.

title: A rainbow patchwork quilt shows agriculture from space | Space photo of the day for June 4, 2026
summary: A composite image from NASA's latest Earth-observing mission showcases a vibrant patchwork of agricultural fields in South Africa. The rainbow-like colors highlight the diversity of crops and land use from space.

title: 'Silo' season 3 trailer teases showing us how the world ended (video)
summary: The trailer for 'Silo' season 3 teases the revelation of how the world ended, with a cryptic message about a new world approaching.

title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is not an alien spacecraft: SETI hunt for 'technosignatures' comes up empty
summary: Scientists found no radio signals from interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, confirming it is a natural object and not an alien spacecraft. The SETI Institute's search for 'technosignatures' yielded no evidence of artificial origin.

title: Meteorite found in Sahara desert may be 1st evidence of lost solar system world
summary: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara Desert provides evidence of a lost moon-size world that existed shortly after the solar system formed. This discovery could offer new insights into the early solar system.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in developing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This advancement is a key milestone for the spacecraft's reusability and future missions.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is evaluating damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for Artemis III have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparation for the Artemis III mission, despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36 (LC-36) with the goal of returning the facility to flight status by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different entities. The launches highlight the growing competition in the satellite internet sector.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. The discovery enhances understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The explosion marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, rebranding existing Commercial Lunar projects. Rovers and drones are being developed to lay the groundwork for the lunar base.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four being SpaceX's Starlink missions. Amazon's Leo missions are also part of the manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the news without additional context provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of milestones in the European space industry.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The team includes young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient technology, likely in the space industry. However, the provided text lacks sufficient context for a detailed analysis.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a significant test involving a parachute bake-out for a space mission. This process likely involves heating the parachute to remove moisture or contaminants before deployment.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life beneath the Martian surface while adhering to planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites in orbit. This innovation could significantly improve space safety and sustainability.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap features insights from CFO and President Ashley Johnson on their differentiated revenue model compared to hardware-focused companies. The discussion highlights how Planet avoids binary outcomes by not relying solely on hardware sales.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx spacecraft on May 19, evaluating parachute performance and vehicle dynamics during a controlled Earth landing. The test used a dedicated drop-test vehicle to simulate the capsule's descent.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch. The satellite is now ready for its upcoming mission.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times the size of its predecessor Condor-XL and targets the emerging orbital-data-center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to revert to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement plan, aligning with industry feedback to guide future lunar missions. This move aims to streamline the process and leverage commercial strategies for lunar payload deliveries.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing launch pad damage. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight timeline in a post on X.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices—SDA and RCO—designed to accelerate space acquisition. This move could significantly impact the Pentagon's efforts to modernize and streamline space programs.

title: Northrop Grumman Taps Apex for Golden Dome SBI Collab
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex to collaborate on the Golden Dome Space-Based Interceptor (SBI) program, aiming to deliver on-orbit missile defense capabilities by 2027.

title: Voyager Will Acquire Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Space Holdings will acquire Astrobotic to enhance its lunar infrastructure capabilities. The acquisition aims to support NASA's Artemis program and sustain human presence on the Moon.

title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has closed a $500 million Series D funding round, focusing on continuing its current trajectory rather than expanding into new ventures or building facilities.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) services for maritime applications. This collaboration aims to enhance connectivity solutions in the maritime sector.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for maritime domain awareness using AI-enabled data analytics. The contract, initially started last year, supports U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites, 'Tom & Jerry', for a rendezvous and inspection mission in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO). The mission aims to demonstrate advanced maneuvering and inspection capabilities.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics and NTT have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on developing a high-speed optical communications infrastructure for space. The partnership aims to enhance space communications capabilities.

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a high-power satellite platform called 'Ultra' designed for orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for 2028.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of satellite propulsion systems.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company is an aerospace and defense firm.

title: Assessing SpaceX Finances, Addressable Market, and the AI Pitch Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX's upcoming IPO has prompted Via Satellite to analyze the company's finances, addressable market, and AI initiatives based on financial filings. Leading space analysts shared insights ahead of the public offering.

title: Impulse Space Raises Another Massive Funding Round in $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has raised a $500 million Series D funding round, led by 137 Ventures and Banner VC. This significant investment marks another major milestone for the company.

title: Iceye U.S. Appoints ASRC Federal Exec Ann Stevens as CEO
summary: Iceye U.S. has appointed Ann Stevens as CEO of its subsidiary, with Eric Jensen transitioning to COO. Stevens joins from ASRC Federal.
